items.title: Safety and accuracy follow different scaling laws in clinical large language models
items.authors: Sebastian Wind, Tri-Thien Nguyen, Jeta Sopa et al.
items.abstract: Clinical LLMs are often scaled by increasing model size, context length, retrieval complexity, or inference-time compute, with the implicit expectation that higher accuracy implies safer behavior. This assumption is incomplete in medicine, where a few confident, high-risk, or evidence-contradicting ...

items.title: OpenSeeker-v2: Pushing the Limits of Search Agents with Informative and High-Difficulty Trajectories
items.authors: Yuwen Du, Rui Ye, Shuo Tang et al.
items.abstract: Deep search capabilities have become an indispensable competency for frontier Large Language Model (LLM) agents, yet their development remains dominated by industrial giants. The typical industry recipe involves a highly resource-intensive pipeline spanning pre-training, continual pre-training (CPT)...

items.title: Audio-Visual Intelligence in Large Foundation Models
items.authors: You Qin, Kai Liu, Shengqiong Wu et al.
items.abstract: Audio-Visual Intelligence (AVI) has emerged as a central frontier in artificial intelligence, bridging auditory and visual modalities to enable machines that can perceive, generate, and interact in the multimodal real world. In the era of large foundation models, joint modeling of audio and vision h...

items.title: UniCorrn: Unified Correspondence Transformer Across 2D and 3D
items.authors: Prajnan Goswami, Tianye Ding, Feng Liu et al.
items.abstract: Visual correspondence across image-to-image (2D-2D), image-to-point cloud (2D-3D), and point cloud-to-point cloud (3D-3D) geometric matching forms the foundation for numerous 3D vision tasks. Despite sharing a similar problem structure, current methods use task-specific designs with separate models ...

items.title: A Closed-Form Adaptive-Landmark Kernel for Certified Point-Cloud and Graph Classification
items.authors: Sushovan Majhi, Atish Mitra, Žiga Virk et al.
items.abstract: We introduce PALACE (Persistence Adaptive-Landmark Analytic Classification Engine), the data-adaptive companion to PLACE, paying a small cross-validation tier on three knobs (budget, radii, bandwidth; $\leq 5$ choices each). A cover-theoretic core (Lebesgue-number criterion on the landmark cover) yi...

items.title: Task-Aware Scanning Parameter Configuration for Robotic Inspection Using Vision Language Embeddings and Hyperdimensional Computing
items.authors: Zhiling Chen, David Gorsich, Matthew P. Castanier et al.
items.abstract: Robotic laser profiling is widely used for dimensional verification and surface inspection, yet measurement fidelity is often dominated by sensor configuration rather than robot motion. Industrial profilers expose multiple coupled parameters, including sampling frequency, measurement range, exposure...

items.title: Evaluating Generative Models as Interactive Emergent Representations of Human-Like Collaborative Behavior
items.authors: Shinas Shaji, Teena Chakkalayil Hassan, Sebastian Houben et al.
items.abstract: Human-AI collaboration requires AI agents to understand human behavior for effective coordination. While advances in foundation models show promising capabilities in understanding and showing human-like behavior, their application in embodied collaborative settings needs further investigation.
